title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are frustrating, especially when permissions haven't changed.

To help us narrow this down: could you walk us through the exact steps you take when you hit the 403? For example:
- Which URL are you navigating to?
- Are you clicking a link from within TaskFlow, using a bookmark, or typing the URL directly?
- When you get the 403, does refreshing the page fix it, or do you have to log out and back in?

Any pattern you've noticed (even if it seems minor) would be really helpful.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I usually just go to the main dashboard URL — I have it bookmarked. Sometimes I click around to different pages too and get the 403 there as well, so it's not specific to one page. When I get a 403, refreshing usually works — sometimes I have to refresh a couple times but eventually it loads. I don't need to log out or anything. Honestly the pattern seems pretty random to me, I figured it might be a network issue or something. It's like maybe 1 out of every 3 times I load a page it happens? Hard to say exactly.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for those details — the fact that refreshing fixes it and it's not tied to a specific page is really useful to know.

Could you share some environment details? Specifically:
- What browser (and version) are you using?
- What OS are you on?
- Do you know which version of TaskFlow your team is running?
- Are you connecting through a VPN, corporate proxy, or any particular network setup?

Since your teammates are seeing it too, knowing whether you're all on the same browser/network would help us figure out if this is tied to infrastructure (like a load balancer) versus something client-side.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm on Chrome, not sure of the exact version — whatever the latest is. Windows 10. I think we're on TaskFlow v2.3.1 but I'm not 100% sure, I'd have to check with our admin. No VPN, just regular office network. I asked a couple teammates and they said they're not having the issue, but I think they have different roles than me — I was just switched to the new 'analyst' role a few days ago. Not sure if that's related.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That detail about the role change is really interesting — you mentioned you were switched to the 'analyst' role a few days ago, which lines up with when the 403s started. A couple of follow-up questions on that:

1. Do you know if the role change happened right around the same time the errors began, or was there a gap?
2. Are there any other users with the 'analyst' role you could check with to see if they're experiencing the same thing?

This would help us figure out whether the issue is tied to how the 'analyst' role's permissions are configured — intermittent 403s that go away on refresh can sometimes point to a caching or session issue where stale permissions are being checked.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, now that you mention it, the timing does line up pretty closely. I got switched to the analyst role and I think the 403s started around the same time — maybe within a day or so? I hadn't really connected those two things until you asked. As for other analysts, I know Sarah on my team also got the analyst role recently. I can ask her if she's seeing the same thing. I'll ping her and let you know. It would make sense if it's a role thing since my teammates who said they weren't having issues are editors and admins.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users with the 'analyst' role","problem":"Users assigned the 'analyst' role receive intermittent 403 Forbidden errors when accessing any page in TaskFlow. The errors occur roughly 1 in 3 page loads, are not tied to a specific URL, and resolve on refresh (sometimes requiring multiple refreshes). Users with 'editor' and 'admin' roles are not affected. The issue began around the time the reporter was switched to the analyst role.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The 'analyst' role's permissions are either misconfigured or are being inconsistently evaluated — most likely a caching issue (e.g., a permissions cache, session cache, or load-balancer-level auth cache) that intermittently serves a stale or incomplete permission set for the analyst role. Since refreshing fixes it, the authorization check likely succeeds on retry when fresh permissions are loaded.","reproduction_steps":["Assign a user the 'analyst' role in TaskFlow","Navigate to the TaskFlow dashboard (or any page) via bookmark or direct URL","Repeat page loads — approximately 1 in 3 loads should return a 403 Forbidden","Refresh the page; it should eventually load successfully"],"environment":"Chrome (latest), Windows 10, TaskFlow ~v2.3.1, standard office network (no VPN or proxy)","severity":"high","impact":"All users with the 'analyst' role are likely affected. The issue disrupts normal workflow — roughly a third of page loads fail, requiring repeated refreshes. Multiple team members may be impacted given that the analyst role was recently rolled out.","recommended_fix":"Investigate the permission/authorization path for the 'analyst' role: (1) Check whether the analyst role's permissions are correctly configured in the RBAC system. (2) Examine any permission caching layer (in-memory cache, Redis, session store) for race conditions or stale entries — the intermittent nature and fix-on-refresh pattern strongly suggest a cache inconsistency. (3) Check if the analyst role was recently added and whether the caching layer properly handles newly-created roles. (4) Review load balancer or reverse proxy configuration for any auth-caching behavior that might not recognize the new role.","proposed_test_case":"Create a test user with the 'analyst' role and programmatically make 50+ sequential requests to the dashboard endpoint, asserting that all return 200. If the bug is present, a significant fraction will return 403. After the fix, all requests should return 200 consistently.","information_gaps":["Exact TaskFlow version (reporter said ~v2.3.1 but was unsure)","Confirmation from another analyst-role user (Sarah) — reporter offered to check but hasn't reported back yet","Server-side logs showing what authorization check is failing on the 403 responses"]}
